description: Codes and standards that govern millwright work — API standards for rotating equipment, ASME standards, ISO vibration and balance standards, AGMA gear standards, alignment tolerances, and OSHA machinery requirements.

API_610.title: Centrifugal Pumps for Petroleum, Petrochemical and Natural Gas Industries

key_requirements.shaft_deflection: Maximum shaft deflection at seal chamber must not exceed 0.002 inch under worst-case conditions
key_requirements.baseplate: Must be rigid enough to maintain alignment. Grouted to foundation.
key_requirements.nozzle_loads: Pump must accept piping loads per API 610 Table 4 without exceeding alignment tolerances
key_requirements.vibration_limits: Unfiltered vibration: 0.12 in/sec velocity at any bearing housing for acceptance test
key_requirements.suction_specific_speed: Below 11,000 (US units) to avoid suction recirculation problems

API_610.practical_note: API 610 is THE standard for refinery pumps. If the spec says 'API 610', every requirement in the standard applies. Read it.

API_686.title: Machinery Installation and Installation Design — THE millwright bible
API_686.covers: Baseplate and sole plate installation, leveling, grouting, alignment, piping connection, pre-commissioning checks

key_tables.alignment_tolerances: Table 1 — offset and angularity limits by RPM. Same as what laser alignment tools use.
key_tables.grout_void: No voids — tap test with hammer to verify solid fill

API_686.practical_note: If you only read one API standard as a millwright, make it API 686. It tells you exactly how to install equipment right the first time.

API_617.title: Axial and Centrifugal Compressors and Expander-compressors
API_617.vibration_limit: Direct (unfiltered): 25.4 * sqrt(12000/RPM) microns, maximum 25.4 microns. Tighter than pumps.

API_611.title: General-Purpose Steam Turbines
API_611.use: Turbines below about 2000 HP. Single-valve, single-stage or multi-stage.

API_612.title: Special-Purpose Steam Turbines
API_612.use: Critical service turbines. Tighter requirements than 611.

API_616.title: Gas Turbines for the Petroleum, Chemical, and Gas Industry Services

ISO_10816.title: Mechanical vibration — Evaluation of machine vibration by measurements on non-rotating parts

zones.Zone_A: Good — newly commissioned or overhauled machine should be in this zone
zones.Zone_B: Acceptable — machine can operate indefinitely with no restrictions
zones.Zone_C: Alert — machine has a problem. Plan corrective action.
zones.Zone_D: Danger — machine may be damaged if operation continues. Shut down.

limits_group_1.description: Large machines over 300 kW on rigid foundations

limits_group_2.description: Medium machines 15-300 kW on rigid foundations

ISO_1940.title: Balance quality requirements for rigid rotors

grades.G6_3: General machinery — fans, pumps, motors, generators
grades.G2_5: Precision machinery — turbines, compressors, precision drives

ISO_1940.formula: Permissible residual unbalance (grams) = (Grade x Weight in kg x 9549) / RPM

AGMA_gear_standards.quality_grades: AGMA 2000: Quality grades 3-15. Higher number = more precise. Industrial gearboxes typically grade 8-10.
AGMA_gear_standards.rating: AGMA 2001 (spur/helical), AGMA 6013 (enclosed drives). Rating considers tooth bending strength and surface durability.
AGMA_gear_standards.lubrication: AGMA 9005: Lubricant selection based on gear type, speed, and operating temperature.

alignment_tolerances.note: 1 mil = 0.001 inch. These are per API 686 Table 1. Tighter is always better — these are MAXIMUM acceptable.
alignment_tolerances.practical_note: For 3600 RPM pump-motor: 2 mil offset and 0.3 mil/inch angularity. Most laser systems display this as a tolerance window. If you are within the box, you pass.

OSHA_machinery.guarding: 29 CFR 1910.219 — All belts, pulleys, gears, shafts, couplings, flywheels, and chains must be guarded if exposed and within 7 feet of floor/platform.
OSHA_machinery.LOTO: 29 CFR 1910.147 — Control of hazardous energy. Written procedures required for each machine. Annual inspection of LOTO procedures.
OSHA_machinery.confined_space: 29 CFR 1910.146 — Permit-required confined spaces. Applies to vessels, tanks, and enclosed equipment.
OSHA_machinery.crane_signals: 29 CFR 1926.1431 — Standard hand signals for crane operations. Signal person must be qualified.
